commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Grok web billing to recognize status-7 credential failures, try browser cookies with a bearer token, reject expired auth-file fallback, parse unframed protobuf responses, and show clearer reauthentication guidance.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main sends cookie-only browser requests and recognizes only RPC status 16 as an authentication failure, matching the reported failure mechanism. The PR does not include a live failing or after-fix account transcript, so runtime reproduction confidence remains medium.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied real-account proof is the remaining merge gate, followed by routine review from the Grok provider owners; automation cannot reproduce the contributor's authenticated setup.

target.title: Fix Grok web billing status 7 auth and cookie bearer fetch
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat gRPC status **7** OAuth2 `bad-credentials` responses as auth failures (in addition to status 16)
- Try **browser cookies + bearer token** before cookie-only when fetching Grok web billing
- Skip expired `~/.grok/auth.json` bearer-only fallback after cookie import fails
- Parse **unframed protobuf** billing payloads when gRPC-web framing is absent
- Surface clearer grok.com reauth guidance in RPC errors and the Grok menu error state

## Problem

Grok web billing could fail with `RPC failed with status 7` even when:
- the user was signed into grok.com in Chrome, and/or
- `~/.grok/auth.json` contained a non-expired token

In practice, grok.com billing often needs the browser session cookie together with a bearer token, and some successful responses arrive as raw protobuf instead of gRPC-web frames.

## Test plan

- [x] `swift test --filter GrokWebBillingFetcherTests`
- [x] `swift test --filter GrokAuthTests`
- [x] `swift test --filter CodexUserFacingErrorTests`
- [x] `make check`
target.author: bcharleson
